title: NOC 21222 – Information Systems Specialists | GTR Immigration
description: Information Systems Specialists are in strong demand across Canada, helping organizations design, audit, modernize, and safeguard enterprise IT environments. Classified under NOC 21222 (TEER 1), this role qualifies for Express-Entry-linked programs, Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs), LMIA-supported work permits, and long-term Permanent Residence (PR) pathways.

classification.category: 2 – Natural and applied sciences and related occupations
classification.teer: 1 – Occupations usually require a university degree
classification.major_group: 212 – Professional occupations in applied sciences (except engineering)

duties: Design, develop, test, and oversee IT systems
Analyze infrastructure data to improve performance
Review existing systems and internal processes
Develop SDLC policies and governance procedures
Identify system discrepancies and recommend fixes
Perform preventive maintenance on computer systems
employment_requirements: Bachelor’s degree in computer science, software engineering, systems engineering, or business administration
Professional certifications such as CISA, CIA, or equivalent IT credentials (preferred by some employers)
job_outlook: This indicates a large immigration-relevant labour market, particularly favourable for candidates with:
additional_information: Demand continues to rise due to:
Digital-transformation projectsCyber-risk governanceEnterprise-system auditsCloud-platform integrationGovernment IT modernization
IT consulting firmsGovernment departmentsFinancial institutionsHealthcare networksTechnology divisions in large corporations
Typical annual earnings range between $48,100–$118,500 CAD, depending on province, experience, and specialization.
